blocks.text: DTC P0299: Insufficient turbocharger boost pressure

The turbocharger system is controlled for boost pressure by the PGM-FI ECU. if the actual boost pressure remains below the specified value for more than a specified period of time in a region where the PGM-FI ECU can determine that sufficient boost pressure is generated, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
When the difference between the upper boost pressure limit and actual boost pressure is 20 kPa (150 mmHg) or more for more than 7.5 seconds.

items.text: West gate control SOL.V. closed sticking
Damaged air tube between compressor and intercooler, air leakage
Characteristic deviation of intake pressure sensor
Characteristic misalignment of atmospheric pressure sensor
Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Engine speed [engine rpm] 3,250 rpm or more and throttle opening 41° or more for at least 7.5 seconds.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
